lesson: c4-l06 / AI for Students with Intellectual Disabilities
title: AI for Students with Intellectual Disabilities

scenes.name: Title
scenes.text: AI for students with intellectual disabilities. [pause] A fundamentally different approach.

scenes.name: The shift
scenes.text: In other lessons, the student uses AI with some independence. [pause] Here, the dynamic changes. [pause] The student works with the mentor. The mentor works with AI. Always layered. Always together.

scenes.name: Principle
scenes.text: The foundational principle. [pause] AI may adapt the <emphasis>method</emphasis> of learning — never the goal. [pause] Simplify language. Break the task into steps. But never complete the task on the student's behalf.

scenes.name: Three uses
scenes.text: Three core uses. [pause] Simplify instructions into concrete language. Break tasks into one-action steps. Create visual and structural supports the mentor can pair with pictograms.

scenes.name: Tools
scenes.text: Tools in the mentor's hand. [pause] ChatGPT or Claude for text simplification. Goblin Tools for task decomposition. ARASAAC for pictograms. [pause] Choiceworks for visual routines.

scenes.name: Rules
scenes.text: Strict rules govern use. [pause] The student does not use AI independently. Less is more — one tool, one purpose. The mentor filters all output. [pause] And AI is never used for assessment.

scenes.name: Dignity
scenes.text: If the student becomes anxious — or pays more attention to the device than the activity — turn the AI off. [pause] The learning continues in the human space between mentor and student.

scenes.name: Privacy
scenes.text: Privacy obligations are heightened. [pause] Never enter personal student data into an AI tool. No names. No diagnoses. [pause] Describe needs in general terms. The technology is for content — nothing more.

scenes.name: Closing
scenes.text: AI in the mentor's hand. [pause] Student in the room.
